---
title: "firestore_preprocessing"
author: "Ken Isaka"
date: "2026-07-02"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

Data preprocessing of .json file from firestore
```{r cars}
# 1. Install required packages (uncomment and run if you don't have them yet)
# install.packages(c("jsonlite", "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr"))

# Load the libraries
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)

# 2. Load the Raw JSON Data
# We use simplifyVector = FALSE to keep the raw list structure intact initially.
# This prevents jsonlite from trying (and failing) to force irregular arrays into matrices.
cat("Loading data from data_pipeline/firestore_export.json...\n")
# You need to change the path is we move the .json file and/or the preprocessing
raw_data <- fromJSON("firestore_export.json", simplifyVector = FALSE)

# 2. Flatten the Users Collection
# Notice we are accessing raw_data$users directly now
users_df <- tibble(
  document_id = names(raw_data$users),
  data = raw_data$users
) %>%
  unnest_wider(data) 

# 3. Flatten the GameLogs Collection
gamelogs_df <- tibble(
  document_id = names(raw_data$gameLogs),
  data = raw_data$gameLogs
) %>%
  unnest_wider(data)

# 4. Join into the Master Dataset
experiment_master <- gamelogs_df %>%
  # Drop treatment and isReplay from gameLogs before joining 
  # so we don't get duplicate '.x' and '.y' columns
  select(-treatment, -isReplay) %>% 
  left_join(users_df, by = "userId") %>%
  # Drop the Firestore document IDs as they aren't needed for analysis
  select(-starts_with("document_id"))

# 5. Verify the data types and structure
glimpse(experiment_master)
View(experiment_master)
```

## Including Plots

Check for randomization frequencies

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# 1. Randomization / Balance Check
cat("--- Covariate Balance --- \n")
balance_check <- experiment_master %>%
  # We only need one row per user to check demographics, not one per round
  distinct(userId, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarize(
    n_users = n(),
    avg_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_quant_exposure = mean(quantitativeExposure, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_puzzle_skill = mean(puzzleSkill, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(balance_check)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
